text: Consider the grammar where S is start symbol, a and b are terminals: S → aSbS | bS | ε. Which statement(s) is/are true?

options.B: The string abb has two distinct derivations
options.C: The string abab has only one rightmost derivation

text: Let M be an NFA with 6 states over a finite alphabet. Which CANNOT be the number of states in the minimal DFA equivalent to M?
Let L₁ and L₂ be two languages over a finite alphabet such that L₁∩L₂ and L₂ are regular. Which statement(s) is/are always true?
Consider CFG G: S → abaABAb bba, A → aaBBA b | bBaba a, B → aBb | ab. For string s∈L(G), let n₁(s)=#a's and n₂(s)=#b's. Which is/are true?

options.A: There is a string s with n₁(s) < n₂(s)
options.B: For every string s, n₁(s) ≥ n₂(s)
options.C: There is a string s with n₁(s) > 2n₂(s)
